subject: Human Resources
title: Employee vs. Independent Contractor
description: A short lesson to help you apply Employee vs. Independent Contractor.

hook.text: Uber classified drivers as contractors. Courts and regulators said: not so fast. The distinction matters legally—and financially.

buildup.text: Employees get benefits, protections, and tax withholding. Contractors get none—but they get autonomy and flexibility.

discovery.text: The IRS uses three factors: behavioral control, financial control, and relationship type to classify workers.

twist.text: Misclassification saves companies 30% in labor costs—but triggers massive penalties, back taxes, and lawsuits when caught.

climax.text: The gig economy blurred the line permanently. New laws like California's AB5 tried to redraw it—with mixed results.

punchline.text: Call them what you want. The law decides what they are.

quiz.question: What determines whether someone is an employee or contractor?
options: Factors like behavioral control, financial control, and relationship type
